title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Spiral Review
summary: Recover from an incorrect edit or commit using the least destructive operation and a verified recovery reference. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.
Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
Create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Undo Layer Choice · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

Which action demonstrates Undo Layer Choice without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.

content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Undo Layer Choice condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Git Reference — Controls porcelain and plumbing command behavior, configuration, revision syntax, hooks, workflows, and recovery.

hints: Name the Undo Layer Choice precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.
There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.

stimulus.title: Reflog Object Recovery case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Reflog entries are shared remote backups that never expire.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work. Artifact under review: an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Reflog Object Recovery, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
